title: Release v1.0 (versioned + DOI, CC-BY content + MIT toolkit), core + advanced set
context: Release v1.0 is the M2 milestone deliverable: a versioned, DOI'd, CC-BY explainer set covering the full core and advanced concept curriculum, accompanied by the MIT interactive toolkit and the checklist deck. This release must meet the full quality bar — every explainer biostatistician-signed-off, 100% reproducible, fully cited, accessibility-passed, and with the 'not clinical advice' frame — before it ships. Only open/synthetic example data with verified licenses are included; no individual-patient data. The release is the first permanently citable, adopter-pinnable version of the library.
objective: Package, version, and publish release v1.0 of the stats-for-clinicians explainer library, covering the core and advanced concept sets, with a DOI, CC-BY-4.0 content license, MIT toolkit license, and a complete release manifest recording specVersions and adoption evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: The release covers the full core set (HR, KM/censoring, median survival, log-rank, Cox, CI vs p-value, absolute vs relative/NNT/NNH) and the advanced/bias set (surrogate endpoints + RMST, immortal-time/lead-time/competing risks, subgroup/multiplicity/non-inferiority/forest plots), plus the checklist deck and interactive toolkit.

Every explainer in the release is biostatistician-signed-off (statsSignoff recorded in the review block), 100% reproducible (repro-005 CI green), fully cited (provgate-015 CI green), accessibility-passed, and carries the structural 'not clinical advice' frame.

Only open/synthetic example data with verified licenses are included; the data manifest is complete for all example datasets; no individual-patient data is present; no copyrighted figures are reproduced.
The release is versioned as v1.0 and receives a DOI (e.g. via Zenodo or equivalent open DOI provider); the DOI is recorded in the release notes.
Content is released under CC-BY-4.0; toolkit code is MIT; redistributed datasets carry their upstream licenses as recorded in the manifest.

Release notes record specVersions: template version, curriculum-map version, toolkit version, each example dataset's snapshot/version and license, and cited-guideline versions (CONSORT/SAMPL/STROBE/ICH E9 editions).
At least one confirmed adopting beneficiary exists (from partner-013) or a documented self-serve adoption by a verifiable third party is recorded as part of the release evidence.
The steward has recorded the release as a delivered outcome artifact (outcomes/v1.0.json or equivalent).
resources: PLAN.md — Definition of Shipped; release and adoption section
output: A published, versioned, DOI'd v1.0 release of the stats-for-clinicians explainer library, with CC-BY-4.0 content, MIT toolkit, complete manifest, 100% reproducible and biostatistician-signed-off content, and a recorded adoption evidence artifact.
